title: fraxinus americana subvar. aucubifolia
intro: Fraxinus americana subvar. aucubifolia, also known as the American white ash or boxleaf ash, is a deciduous tree native to North America. The leaves and bark of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus americana subvar. aucubifolia include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
